context.projectOverview: agent-bober telegram adapter. Sprints 1-5 DONE. Funnel is now UNIFIED: src/telegram/outbound.ts has sendSafe (text) + sendSafeKeyboard (keyboards) — the SOLE chokepoints; BotTransport (bot.ts, grammy only) has sendMessage/sendKeyboard/answerCallback/downloadDocument. Sprint 6 adds TWO delivery modes over the funnel: (a) streaming — edit ONE status message in place per progress event (needs editMessage on the transport); (b) silent digest — send with disable_notification:true. NO run/fleet/scheduler logic — presentation only.

completedSprints.title: transport+whitelist+funnel
completedSprints.summary: sendSafe; grammy behind BotTransport in bot.ts.

completedSprints.title: upload opt-in + FUNNEL UNIFICATION
completedSprints.summary: handlers/upload.ts (per-upload Yes/No medical opt-in, count-only reply); outbound.ts gained sendSafeKeyboard = the single keyboard chokepoint; BotTransport gained downloadDocument; grammy still bot.ts-only.

relevantFiles: src/telegram/outbound.ts (sendSafe + sendSafeKeyboard — EXTEND to carry delivery options: silent/edit-target, OR add sendSafe variants; keep the single-chokepoint discipline)
src/telegram/bot.ts (BotTransport — add editMessage + disable_notification option on sendMessage; grammy only here)
src/telegram/handlers/*.ts (injected-dep + injected-spy test pattern to mirror)
do-bridge run path (src/do-bridge/) for the manual streaming wiring — read-only progress signals, NO run logic

contract.title: Streaming run progress (in-place edits) + silent scheduled digest delivery
contract.description: Add two outbound delivery modes over the sendSafe funnel: (a) streaming — a long-running operation reports progress by editing one status message in place as progress events arrive, ending with a final summary; (b) silent digest — a scheduler-handed digest payload is sent with notifications disabled. No run, fleet, or scheduler logic is added; this remains a presentation adapter.

successCriteria.description: `npm run build` (tsc) compiles the streaming and digest delivery additions with zero TypeScript errors.

A unit test shows the streaming sender, given a sequence of N>=2 progress updates, issues one send call and at least two edit calls on the same message id (verified via an injected transport spy) rather than one new message per update.
A unit test shows the digest sender sets the disable_notification flag on the outgoing message.
A unit test shows both the streaming and digest senders route their content through the sendSafe funnel.
Triggering a long do-bridge run shows a status message in Telegram that updates in place at least twice and ends with a final result; a delivered digest arrives without a notification sound.

nonGoals: Do not implement the scheduler itself — this sprint only delivers a digest payload handed to it; scheduling cadence is owned elsewhere.
Do not add run or fleet execution logic — read existing run progress signals only; this stays a presentation adapter.
Do not send sensitive run detail — status updates and digests carry summaries only via sendSafe.
Do not post a new message per progress tick — edit a single status message in place.
Do not bypass the whitelist or the sendSafe funnel.
stopConditions: The streaming (one send, multiple edits) and silent-flag unit tests pass and `npm run build` exits zero.
A digest send sets disable_notification and a streamed run produces in-place edits rather than repeated new messages.
contract.definitionOfDone: Long-running operations report progress by editing one Telegram status message in place as progress events arrive, ending with a final summary, instead of posting a new message per tick. Scheduled digests are delivered with notifications silenced (disable_notification). Both paths emit only non-sensitive summaries through the sendSafe funnel, and neither adds run, fleet, or scheduler logic.
assumptions: Run progress is read from existing signals (e.g. history.jsonl events / telemetry) without adding run logic; the streaming sender consumes an async iterable / callback of progress updates supplied by the caller.
The Telegram transport wrapper from Sprint 1 exposes editMessage and a disable_notification option on sendMessage (libraries supporting getUpdates expose both).
A 'digest payload' is plain summary text handed in by the scheduler owner; this adapter only sends it silently.
outOfScope: Deciding what goes into a digest or when it fires (scheduler, owned elsewhere).
Token-level streaming from the model — progress is event/step granularity, not per-token.
contract.generatorNotes: Add src/telegram/streaming.ts (streamProgress(transport, chatId, updates): send an initial status message, then call editMessage on that message id for each subsequent update, final edit = summary) and src/telegram/digest.ts (sendDigest(transport, chatId, text): send via sendSafe with disable_notification: true). Both go through the sendSafe funnel from Sprint 1 (extend the funnel to accept delivery options like silent/edit-target rather than calling the transport directly). Source progress generically: accept an injected async iterable or onUpdate callback so the unit test drives a fixed sequence; for the manual path, tail existing run progress (history.jsonl/telemetry) — do not add run logic. Wire so a do-bridge promotion run (resolved via Sprint 4's gate) reports via streamProgress. ESM .js imports, `import type`, no `any`, node:fs/promises only.
contract.evaluatorNotes: With an injected transport spy, drive >=2 progress updates and assert exactly one send + >=2 edits on the same message id. Assert sendDigest sets disable_notification true. Assert both paths call sendSafe (no direct transport send). Confirm no run/fleet/scheduler logic was added (diff stays within src/telegram/). Run build + the streaming/digest unit tests.

principles:
# Project Principles

> These principles guide all planning, generation, and evaluation in the Bober pipeline.
> Updated: 2026-04-16

## Mission

Agent-bober is a multi-agent harness that builds applications autonomously using any LLM. It orchestrates a pipeline of specialized agents (Researcher, Planner, Curator, Generator, Evaluator) and supports Claude, GPT, Gemini, and Ollama. It ships as an npm CLI + MCP server for Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf.

## Users

- **Primary:** Developers using Claude Code, Cursor, or Windsurf who want autonomous feature generation with quality gates
- **Secondary:** Teams integrating agent-bober programmatically via the TypeScript API or MCP server
- Technical level: intermediate to advanced — users understand LLMs, CI/CD, and modern JS tooling

## Quality Standards

- **Type safety:** TypeScript strict mode with all strict flags (`noUnusedLocals`, `noUnusedParameters`, `noImplicitReturns`, `noFallthroughCasesInSwitch`, `isolatedModules`). Zero type errors is a hard gate.
- **Lint compliance:** ESLint flat config with `consistent-type-imports` enforced, `no-explicit-any` warned, unused vars errored (with `_` prefix escape). Zero lint errors is a hard gate.
- **Test coverage:** Vitest for unit tests. Tests are collocated with source (`*.test.ts` next to `*.ts`). Tests run against the real project when practical (e.g., scanner tests scan agent-bober itself).
- **Build integrity:** `tsc` must produce clean output. Build is a required evaluator strategy.

## Technical Principles

### Follow

- **ESM everywhere.** `"type": "module"` in package.json. All imports use `.js` extensions for NodeNext resolution. No CommonJS.
- **Provider-agnostic interfaces.** All LLM interaction goes through `providers/types.ts` — provider-specific SDKs are wrapped by adapters (`anthropic.ts`, `openai.ts`, `google.ts`). Never leak SDK types outside adapter files.
- **Zod for config validation.** All configuration schemas are Zod schemas in `config/schema.ts`. Runtime config loading uses `z.parse()`. No hand-rolled validation.
- **Contract-based architecture.** PlanSpecs, SprintContracts, and EvalResults are the communication protocol between agents. They live in `contracts/` and are JSON files on disk in `.bober/`.
- **Filesystem state.** All mutable state (specs, contracts, handoffs, eval results, history) is stored as JSON files in `.bober/`. No database, no in-memory global state.
- **Section comments.** Use unicode box-drawing section headers: `// -- Section Name ------` to organize long files.
- **Small utility modules.** Utils in `utils/` are focused single-purpose files (`fs.ts`, `git.ts`, `logger.ts`). Keep them small.
- **Conventional commits.** Format: `type: description` where type is `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `release`, `refactor`, `test`, `chore`. Sprint-generated commits use `bober(sprint-N): description`.
- **Use `type` imports.** ESLint enforces `consistent-type-imports` -- import types with `import type { ... }` syntax.
- **Prefix unused params with `_`.** The `_` prefix is the only escape hatch for unused variables/parameters.

### Avoid

- **No `any` without justification.** `no-explicit-any` is a warning; aim for zero. Use `unknown` + type narrowing instead.
- **No SDK lock-in.** Never import `@anthropic-ai/sdk` or `openai` outside of their respective adapter files in `providers/`.
- **No synchronous filesystem ops.** All fs operations use `node:fs/promises`. No `fs.readFileSync` etc.
- **No barrel re-exports for deep internals.** `src/index.ts` exports the public API only. Internal modules import directly.
- **No test mocks for filesystem.** Tests that need filesystem state create temp directories and clean up. The scanner tests run against the real codebase.

## Design Principles

N/A -- no user interface. Agent-bober is a CLI tool and programmatic library. The MCP server exposes tools, not UI.
